title: lichen_l2.c: 'python-xxx' comment references are opaque
description:
Comments in lichen_l2.c reference 'python-ano.27', 'python-t7j5.1', 'python-t7j5.4', 'python-qhvc.3' (lines 67, 139-141, 203-204, 321, 357) without explanation. These appear to be internal tracking IDs from a review or issue tracker, but they're not actionable without access to that system.

What goes wrong: A reader cannot understand what these references mean or why the code exists without external context. If the tracking system is lost, these comments become meaningless cruft.

Fix: Replace the IDs with inline rationale (e.g., 'Guards against access before initialization' instead of just the ID), or add a top-of-file comment explaining how to look up the IDs.

Locations:
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:67
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:139-141
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:203-204
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:321
- firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lichen_l2.c:357
